claims.label: Definition 2.1 (Operator coherence principle)
claims.statement: A state Psi in Dom(A) is exactly coherent relative to A if there exists E in R such that A Psi = E Psi; it is near-coherent when it is close to that relation in norm.
claims.notes: This definition sets coherence as an operator property rather than a descriptive label.

claims.label: Definition 5.1 (Residual functional)
claims.statement: For Psi in Dom(A), residual coherence is defined by R(Psi) = inf over E in R of ||A Psi - E Psi||.
claims.notes: This is the central mathematical object of the framework.

claims.label: Proposition 4.2 (Separated coherent modes)
claims.statement: For the quadratic local model U(s) = kappa (s-s_*)^2, separated product states Psi_{ell,alpha,n}(g,s) = Y_{ell,alpha}(g) chi_n(s) satisfy A Psi_{ell,alpha,n} = E_{ell,n} Psi_{ell,alpha,n} with E_{ell,n} = lambda_ell + mu_n.
claims.proof_sketch: Apply the product operator to Y_{ell,alpha}(g) chi_n(s), use the S^3 eigenvalue equation and the shifted harmonic-oscillator eigenvalue equation, and collect scalar terms.
claims.notes: This establishes exact coherent families in the local v0.1 model.

claims.label: Proposition 5.2 (Meaning of residual size)
claims.statement: For nonzero Psi in Dom(A), R(Psi)=0 if and only if Psi is an exact eigenstate of A; small R(Psi) indicates near-coherence, while large R(Psi) indicates non-coherence relative to A.
claims.proof_sketch: Use the definition of R(Psi): eigenstates make the norm vanish directly; conversely, if the infimum is zero, one obtains convergence to an eigenvalue relation.

claims.label: Lemma 5.3 (Best scalar fit)
claims.statement: For nonzero Psi, the minimizing scalar in ||A Psi - E Psi||^2 is E_*(Psi) = <Psi, A Psi> / ||Psi||^2, and R(Psi)^2 equals ||A Psi||^2 minus |<Psi, A Psi>|^2 / ||Psi||^2.
claims.proof_sketch: Expand the quadratic in E, differentiate, and substitute the minimizer.
claims.notes: This gives a computable residual form tied to the Rayleigh quotient.

claims.label: Proposition 6.1 (Spectral decay law)
claims.statement: Under the dissipative flow d_t Psi = -A Psi, each spectral coefficient c_{ell,alpha,n}(t) decays as c_{ell,alpha,n}(0) exp(-E_{ell,n} t), so higher-energy components decay faster.
claims.proof_sketch: Expand the initial state in the eigenbasis and solve uncoupled scalar ODEs for coefficients.
claims.notes: This formalizes temporal equilibration in the v0.1 setting.

claims.label: Corollary 6.2 (Suppression of high-energy sectors)
claims.statement: If E_i > E_j and c_j(0) is nonzero, then |c_i(t)| / |c_j(t)| decays exponentially to zero as t increases.
claims.proof_sketch: Take the ratio of coefficient decay laws from Proposition 6.1.

claims.label: Interpretive Claim 7.1 (Operational metric backbone)
claims.statement: The residual and auxiliary coherence measures define a common metric layer that can guide alignment scoring, residual minimization, mode concentration tracking, and convergence diagnostics across RQM Studio, WaveEngine, and future coherence-locked systems.
claims.notes: This is a systems-design interpretation, not an empirical performance claim.
